response_text:
To find the distance between the two cars after 2 hours, you can calculate the distance each car travels individually and then find the difference:

1.  **Calculate the distance of the first car:**
    $60 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = 120 \text{ miles}$

2.  **Calculate the distance of the second car:**
    $70 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = 140 \text{ miles}$

3.  **Find the difference:**
    $140 \text{ miles} - 120 \text{ miles} = 20 \text{ miles}$

Alternatively, you can calculate the **relative speed** between the two cars:
$70 \text{ mph} - 60 \text{ mph} = 10 \text{ mph}$ (the second car gains 10 miles every hour).
$10 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = \mathbf{20 \text{ miles}}.$

After 2 hours, the cars will be **20 miles** apart.
